parsed_review.summary: CP00-698 is a descriptor-only RP23 finance-integrations P02 implementation/workflow pack (40 units, RP23.P02.M03.S01-RP23.P02.M04.S18, risk B). The pack-level claim is honest and supported: manifest production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, descriptor_only=true, and construction-inspection/adjudication/command-evidence/claude-review-result are uniformly pending_review with no-real-data and runtime-closed dispositions. Independent source inspection confirms the boundaries are enforced, not merely asserted: registry.js FINANCE_INTEGRATIONS_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION and service.js ROW_EXTRAS set every write/persistence/runtime/credential/audit flag to false; validators.js validateDescriptor rejects any row that is not descriptor_only, executes runtime, includes raw/financial/credential payloads, opens bank/card/WEHAGO/Douzone/tax/DART runtime, exposes permission decisions or cross-tenant access, exposes audit bodies, or loads real financial data, and requires authority_boundary.human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false. model.test.js asserts CP698 coverage equals requirements.deliverable_counts, runtime-closed packets, Claude read_only, Hermes emits_runtime_receipt=false, handoff to CP00-699, and runtime_opening_pack_id=CP00-699. The generated contract projection has latest_pack CP00-698, validation.valid=true, and zero leak-fields set true; a secret/PII/credential sweep matched only negation guards. I tallied all 40 units: contract 2, implementation 18, security_audit 4, ui 6, claude_review 2, failure_recovery 4, test 4, micro-titles 22/18 — exact match to the baseline. Claude is preserved as a read-only, non-final reviewer and Hermes is used only for deterministic review-packet/receipt handling, not product runtime. Provenance: I did not re-run the validation matrix (read-only, no Bash); I relied on command-evidence.json exit codes (reported 23/23 / all exit 0) plus direct code and test inspection. One non-blocking P3 bookkeeping inconsistency in the scope file is noted. No P0/P1/P2 findings; pack is closeout-eligible and does not block this CP's goal closeout.

findings.title: changed-file-scope bookkeeping: new-session-handoff.md dual-listed and cp00-698 evidence dir not in active_pack_required_untracked_files
findings.details: docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md appears in both untracked_implementation_files (line 181) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (line 188), and docs/closeout-packs/cp00-698/ is listed in untracked_implementation_files (line 182) while active_pack_required_untracked_files is empty ([]) even though the cp00-698 evidence directory is a required, currently-untracked pack artifact. This is purely scope-tool metadata and does not affect the pack: new-session-handoff.md is correctly excluded from active_pack_implementation_refs, active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty, and all required cp00-698 evidence files exist on disk and were inspected. No deliverable, boundary, or production_ready claim is impacted.
findings.recommendation: Non-blocking. Optionally tighten the scope generator so a file is partitioned into exactly one of untracked_implementation_files / unrelated_dirty_files_preserved, and so required untracked pack-evidence directories are reflected in active_pack_required_untracked_files. No change required for CP00-698 closeout.
